title: SPARQL: FILTER bound with undefined
description:
select ?v1 ?v2 where
{
  values (?v1 ?v2)
  {
     (1 "one")
     (2 "two")
     (3 UNDEF)
  }
  FILTER (bound(?v2)).
}

returns three rows instead of the expected two (the last row where ?v2 is unbound should not be included in the resultset, since it does not satisfy the filtering condition):
v1  v2
1   one
2   two
3
However, the following query with the double negation produces the expected result:
select ?v1 ?v2 where
{
  values (?v1 ?v2)
  {
     (1 "one")
     (2 "two")
     (3 UNDEF)
  }
  FILTER (!!bound(?v2)).
}


v1  v2
1   one
2   two
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), value INT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'one', 10), (2, 'two', 20), (3, NULL);
SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE NOT (value IS NULL);
SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value > 15);
key_bug_features: FILTER bound with undefined variable in SPARQL query
